*Name:* Pitsligo Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 57.6923, -2.1069
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pitsligo_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Pitsligo%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/Pitsligo_Castle_-_geograph.org.uk_-_7912882.jpg/330px-Pitsligo_Castle_-_geograph.org.uk_-_7912882.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* Google places is wrong Pittulie and Pitsligo castles are nearby but two different castles. 

*Description:*
Pitsligo Castle is a ruined castle half a mile east of Rosehearty, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Modified in the 1570s by the Forbes of Druminnor, it was described by W. Douglas Simpson as one of the nine castles of the Knuckle, referring to the rocky headland of North-East Aberdeenshire. It is listed by Historic Environment Scotland as a scheduled monument.

_Listing 1 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Cairnbulg Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 57.6656, -1.9736
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cairnbulg_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Cairnbulg%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ca/Cairnbulg_Castle.jpg/330px-Cairnbulg_Castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* Needs more research 

*Description:*
Cairnbulg Castle is a z-plan castle situated in Cairnbulg, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It was described by W. Douglas Simpson as one of the nine castles of the Knuckle, referring to the rocky headland of north-east Aberdeenshire. It stands by the River Philorth and was originally known as Philorth Castle. The 17th-century Philorth Castle, an L-plan house consisting of a sizeable crow-stepped block, was demolished after a fire in 1915.

_Listing 3 of 10_

The 17th-century Philorth Castle, an L-plan house consisting of a sizeable crow-stepped block, was demolished after a fire in 1915.

*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 41.5565, -72.8354
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Castle_Craig
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Craig%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/Castle_Craig.jpg/330px-Castle_Craig.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* Needs more research. wiki coordinates are in connecticut? 

*Description:*
Castle Craig, located on East Peak in the Hanging Hills, is constructed of trap rock and is 32 feet (10 m) in height and has a base 58 feet (18 m) in circumference. A metal interior stairway is used to ascend the observation deck on top. It stands 976 feet (297 m) above sea level and provides an expansive view of the greater Meriden, Connecticut area.

_Listing 1 of 2_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Craig y Nos Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* mid wales marches
*County:* Powys
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 19th century
*Coords:* 51.825, -3.68416667
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Craig-y-Nos_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Craig%20y%20Nos%20Castle/@51.8361,-3.6861,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqF41aMvBoFGOgpoxdeAbnxZIVMIPdE-1FYlkdCf2urBwrzVMia6p79g0hV2E30yP4agakn4b-rhz_NHL8MZOwZdWEMrkpvGdbI=s4800-w719
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* mark as hotel. 

*Description:*
Craig-y-nos Castle, is a Scots baronial-style country house near Glyntawe in Powys, Wales. Built on parkland beside the River Tawe in the upper Swansea Valley, it is located on the southeastern edge of the Black Mountain. The castle, formerly owned by opera singer Adelina Patti, is now a hotel. Its landscaped grounds are now a country park, managed by the Brecon Beacons National Park Authority. They are listed on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales. The castle is a Grade II* listed building and its theatre has a separate Grade I listing.

_Listing 2 of 2_

listing.name: Craig y Nos Castle
listing.desc: Craig-y-nos Castle, is a Scots baronial-style country house near Glyntawe in Powys, Wales. Built on parkland beside the River Tawe in the upper Swansea Valley, it is located on the southeastern edge of the Black Mountain. The castle, formerly owned by opera singer Adelina Patti, is now a hotel. Its landscaped grounds are now a country park, managed by the Brecon Beacons National Park Authority. They are listed on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales. The castle is a Grade II* listed building and its theatre has a separate Grade I listing.

imageAttribution.description: Craig-y-nos Castle — a 19th century Gothic revival style castle in Wales.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Inverallochy Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.65641, -1.93308
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverallochy_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Inverallochy%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e4/Inverallochy_Castle_-_geograph.org.uk_-_8145389.jpg/330px-Inverallochy_Castle_-_geograph.org.uk_-_8145389.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Inverallochy Castle is a ruined courtyard castle, near the village of Inverallochy in the Buchan area of North-East Scotland, dating to 1504. It lies 0.5 miles (0.80 km) south of Cairnbulg Castle, near Fraserburgh, and formerly stood beside the now-drained Loch of Inverallochy. It was described by W. Douglas Simpson as one of the nine castles of the Knuckle, referring to the rocky headland of North-East Aberdeenshire.

_Listing 2 of 10_

listing.desc: Inverallochy Castle is a ruined courtyard castle, near the village of Inverallochy in the Buchan area of North-East Scotland, dating to 1504. Douglas Simpson as one of the nine castles of the Knuckle, referring to the rocky headland of North-East Aberdeenshire.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Kinnaird Head Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.69754, -2.00399
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kinnaird_Head
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Kinnaird%20Head%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqHFAtgwBMbYP_9ijlyof9eLs0gAgG0Dce6lbFUJqjVBvzNC5kfX8JBwPvsSsu-8ldq6jSNeSTse4E8rFlrdtgHKGS0kFjIkB0o=s4800-w768
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Kinnaird Head is a headland projecting into the North Sea, within the town of Fraserburgh, Aberdeenshire, on the east coast of Scotland. The 16th-century Kinnaird Castle was converted in 1787 for use as the Kinnaird Head Lighthouse, the first lighthouse in Scotland to be lit by the Commissioners of Northern Lights. Kinnaird Castle and the nearby Wine Tower were described by W. Douglas Simpson as two of the nine castles of the Knuckle, referring to the rocky headland of north-east Aberdeenshire. The lighthouse is a category A listed building. and the Wine Tower. is a scheduled monument. The buildings around the base of the lighthouse are the work of Robert Stevenson.

_Listing 3 of 10_

listing.name: Kinnaird Head Castle
listing.desc: Kinnaird Head is a headland projecting into the North Sea, within the town of Fraserburgh, Aberdeenshire, on the east coast of Scotland. The 16th-century Kinnaird Castle was converted in 1787 for use as the Kinnaird Head Lighthouse, the first lighthouse in Scotland to be lit by the Commissioners of Northern Lights. Kinnaird Castle and the nearby Wine Tower were described by W. Douglas Simpson as two of the nine castles of the Knuckle, referring to the rocky headland of north-east Aberdeenshire. The lighthouse is a category A listed building. and the Wine Tower. is a scheduled monument. The buildings around the base of the lighthouse are the work of Robert Stevenson.

imageAttribution.artist: Stanley Bruce (User: Bard o' the Broch)
imageAttribution.description: Fraserburgh Lighthouse (Kinnaird Castle) and the Wine Tower

Balquhain Castle is a ruined tower house in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It served as the stronghold of the Leslies of Balquhain. The castle is located 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) to the west of Inverurie, and is protected as a scheduled monument.
